title: lanugothamnus leucocephalus
side_effects: Lanugothamnus leucocephalus can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, due to its high toxicity levels. Prolonged consumption of the plant can also lead to kidney damage and liver failure, as it contains compounds that can cause oxidative stress and inflammation in the body. Furthermore, using Lanugothamnus leucocephalus in excess can cause neurological symptoms like seizures, tremors, and even coma in severe cases. Ingesting the plant's seeds or other parts can exacerbate its side effects, posing a significant risk to human health.
